texto: It has taken almost half my life away from Ireland for me to truly feel what home really is, and it is not what I was expecting. In the end it was not a place, or a past, or any sort of single, dazzling epiphany. It was all the little things. Cold butter spread thick on sweet wheaten bread or hot, subsiding potatoes; the scent of wet, black soil; a bushy spine of grass on a one-track road; wide iron gates leading to high beech corridors; the chalky smell of a cow's wet muzzle, and, most of all, in Seamus Heaney's words, the sound of rivers in the trees.
explicacao: Citação de Trish Deseine sobre sentido da existência e experiência vivida. “It has taken almost half my life away from Ireland for me to truly feel what home really is, and …” articula reflexao, food, home em registro de reflexao, com relevância para contextos como termino e viagem.
